seo.title: 100 Self-Hosting resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Self-Hosting resources for engineers.

content.intro: Self-hosting allows developers to reclaim control over data sovereignty, reduce recurring SaaS costs, and optimize performance by deploying closer to end-users. This resource guide focuses on production-ready tools and workflows for managing containerized applications on independent infrastructure like Hetzner or Contabo.

items.title: Coolify
items.description: An open-source, self-hosted alternative to Heroku/Netlify. It supports Docker, Nixpacks, and static sites with built-in database management and automated SSL.

items.title: Dokku
items.description: A Docker-powered mini-PaaS that uses Heroku-style buildpacks. Ideal for single-server setups where you want to deploy via 'git push dokku main'.

items.title: CapRover
items.description: An easy-to-use app/database deployment tool with a web UI. Supports One-Click apps for instant deployment of WordPress, Meilisearch, and more.

items.title: Kamal
items.description: A tool by 37signals for deploying web apps in Docker containers to any host using SSH. It handles zero-downtime deploys and rolling restarts without a complex PaaS layer.

items.title: Portainer CE
items.description: A lightweight management UI that allows you to manage Docker environments, stacks, and containers across multiple nodes through a web interface.

items.title: Hetzner Cloud
items.description: High-performance VPS provider based in Germany/Finland. Recommended for GDPR compliance and the best price-to-performance ratio for European traffic.

items.title: PikaPods
items.description: A managed hosting service for open-source apps. Useful for developers who want the 'self-hosted' software stack without managing the underlying Linux OS.

items.title: Nomad by HashiCorp
items.description: A flexible scheduler and orchestrator that can deploy a mix of microservices, batch jobs, and containerized applications across a cluster.

items.title: Cosmos Cloud
items.description: A self-hosting platform that includes a secure reverse proxy, container management, and a built-in authentication provider with 2FA/WebAuthn.

items.title: Taisun
items.description: A web-based Docker management tool designed for home servers and small VPS instances, focusing on ease of use and stack templates.

items.title: Caddy Server
items.description: A modern web server that automates SSL certificate issuance via Let's Encrypt by default. Use it for its simple Caddyfile syntax and performance.

items.title: Traefik Proxy
items.description: A cloud-native edge router that integrates with Docker labels to automatically route traffic to containers as they spin up.

items.title: Nginx Proxy Manager
items.description: A GUI-based tool for managing Nginx proxy hosts, offering a simplified way to handle SSL, redirects, and access lists.

items.title: Authentik
items.description: An open-source Identity Provider that adds SSO and MFA to apps that don't natively support them. Use the Proxy Provider for legacy apps.

items.title: CrowdSec
items.description: A modern, collaborative alternative to Fail2Ban that parses logs to detect and block malicious IPs across your entire infrastructure.

items.title: Tailscale
items.description: A zero-config mesh VPN based on WireGuard. Use it to access your internal services securely without exposing ports to the public internet.

items.title: Cloudflare Tunnels
items.description: Expose local web servers to the internet without opening firewall ports. Traffic is routed through Cloudflare's edge for DDoS protection.

items.title: Authelia
items.description: A lightweight authentication and authorization server providing 2FA and SSO for your applications via a reverse proxy like Nginx or Traefik.

items.title: UFW (Uncomplicated Firewall)
items.description: The standard firewall tool for Ubuntu. Always set to 'default deny incoming' and only open ports 80, 443, and your SSH port.

items.title: BunkerWeb
items.description: A next-generation web application firewall (WAF) that integrates with Docker to protect your web services from common attacks automatically.

items.title: Watchtower
items.description: A container that monitors your running Docker containers and automatically updates them when a new image version is pushed to the registry.

items.title: Uptime Kuma
items.description: A self-hosted monitoring tool like UptimeRobot. Supports multiple notification methods including Discord, Telegram, and Gotify.

items.title: Rclone
items.description: The 'Swiss army knife' of cloud storage. Use it in a cron job to sync your Docker volume backups to S3-compatible storage or Google Drive.

items.title: BorgBackup
items.description: A deduplicating backup program. Essential for self-hosters to keep encrypted, space-efficient snapshots of application data.

items.title: Netdata
items.description: Provides real-time performance monitoring for Linux systems. Install it to visualize CPU, RAM, and Disk I/O spikes in high resolution.

items.title: Dozzle
items.description: A simple, lightweight web-based log viewer for Docker containers. Faster than running 'docker logs' via CLI for quick debugging.

items.title: Healthchecks.io (Self-hosted)
items.description: Monitors your cron jobs and background tasks. If a backup script fails to 'ping' this service, you get an immediate alert.

items.title: Glances
items.description: A cross-platform monitoring tool that provides a comprehensive overview of system resources in a single terminal or web page.

items.title: Prometheus & Grafana
items.description: The industry standard for metrics collection and visualization. Best for multi-server setups or complex application monitoring.

items.title: Scrutiny
items.description: A hard drive S.M.A.R.T monitoring dashboard. Critical for developers running self-hosted apps on physical hardware or dedicated servers.

pro_tips: Always use Docker volumes for persistent data; never store application state inside the container's writable layer.
Implement a 3-2-1 backup strategy: 3 copies of data, 2 different media, 1 offsite (e.g., Hetzner storage box or S3).
Use a dedicated management network via Tailscale or WireGuard to access SSH and admin UIs, keeping them off the public internet.
Hard-limit container resources (CPU/RAM) in your docker-compose files to prevent a single runaway process from crashing the entire VPS.
Automate security patches for the host OS using 'unattended-upgrades' on Debian/Ubuntu systems to mitigate kernel-level vulnerabilities.
